system_01.478: {PC:00:TIDUS}{\n}I think we can make it!{TIME:B9}{PAUSE}{PC:01:YUNA}{\n}We will make it!

bika01_10.1: {PC:06:RIKKU}{\n}I can take them{\n}apart piece by piece!

bjyt04_01.2: {TIME:00}Certain items, like {CLR:YELLOW}grenades{CLR:WHITE}, have{\n}special effects when used.
bjyt04_01.3: {TIME:00}These items can only be used during battle{\n}by selecting the {CLR:YELLOW}Special{CLR:WHITE} ability {CLR:YELLOW}Use{CLR:WHITE}.

bsil05_00.1: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}You’d best stop that. {PC:04:WAKKA}’s{\n}in charge of flyers, remember?
bsil05_00.3: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}Didn’t I just tell you?{\n}You need magic to harm that one.
bsil05_00.5: {PC:04:WAKKA}{\n}It’ll take more than{\n}one shot to do the job.
bsil05_00.6: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}Why don’t you let the{\n}boy handle that one?
bsil05_00.11: {PC:04:WAKKA}{\n}Excuse me? I’m in charge{\n}of swatting those, ya?
bsil05_00.12: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}Just thought I’d give it a try!
bsil05_00.14: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}Let us guardians handle{\n}the likes of these.
bsil05_00.18: {PC:04:WAKKA}{\n}If you get hurt, get{\n}{PC:01:YUNA} to fix you up.

bsil07_50.1: {PC:04:WAKKA}{\n}Hey, why don’t ya try out{\n}that sword I gave you?
bsil07_50.3: {PC:04:WAKKA}{\n}Not too shabby! You kept{\n}up with him pretty well.

bsil07_51.2: {PC:04:WAKKA}{\n}Told you. Only magic{\n}can beat that thing.
bsil07_51.3: {PC:04:WAKKA}{\n}If you can’t beat ’em down, you gotta{\n}use magic of an element they don’t like.
bsil07_51.5: {PC:04:WAKKA}{\n}Let’s have our black mage{\n}show you what I mean.
bsil07_51.8: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}Elements are the essence{\n}of both magic and fiends.
bsil07_51.9: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}Fire and ice, lighting and{\n}water--four elements in all.
bsil07_51.10: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}The fire element and{\n}ice element are opposed.
bsil07_51.11: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}Spells of ice work well{\n}against fire fiends...
bsil07_51.12: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}And ice fiends are{\n}weak against fire magic.
bsil07_51.13: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}The elements of fire and ice are{\n}two sides of the same coin.
bsil07_51.15: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}There are four elements:{\n}fire, ice, lightning, and water.
bsil07_51.16: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}Fire fiends are weak against ice magic,{\n}and ice fiends are weak against fire.
bsil07_51.18: {PC:00:TIDUS}{\n}I get fire and ice, but what{\n}about lightning and water?
bsil07_51.19: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}Lightning and water are opposed,{\n}just like fire and ice.
bsil07_51.20: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}This one here is a{\n}water fiend, which means...
bsil07_51.21: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}If you hit a water fiend with a{\n}lightning attack it should only take one hit.
bsil07_51.23: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}This one’s a water fiend, so{\n}fire and ice aren’t very effective.
bsil07_51.24: {PC:04:WAKKA}{\n}Whoa! Hey! What are you{\n}healing that thing for?
bsil07_51.25: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}This is the sort of thing that can{\n}happen if you pick the wrong element.
bsil07_51.29: {PC:04:WAKKA}{\n}Man, just use the{\n}Thunder on that thing, ya?
bsil07_51.31: {TIME:00}{CLR:WHITE}Magic and fiends have properties called {CLR:YELLOW}elements{CLR:WHITE}.{\n}There are four elements: fire, ice, lightning, and water.{\n}Fire and ice are opposed, as are lightning and water.

genk00_40.3: Belgemine{\n}Don’t hold anything back.{\n}Show me what you’ve learned!
genk00_40.4: Belgemine{\n}You have a lot to learn.
genk00_40.6: {TIME:00}Note that this Ixion used ”Haste.”{\n}How is it that it may use magic{\n}that {PC:01:YUNA}’s {PC:0A:IXION} cannot?

genk16_50.2: {PC:06:RIKKU}{\n}You should know, I’m{\n}scary when I get mad!
genk16_50.3: {PC:06:RIKKU}{\n}I’m gonna use that item I just{\n}got on you! You’ll be sorry!
genk16_50.10: {PC:06:RIKKU}{\n}I can combine items{\n}and use them like this.
genk16_50.14: {TIME:00}{CLR:WHITE}Use the {CLR:YELLOW}Special{CLR:WHITE} ability {CLR:YELLOW}Steal{CLR:WHITE} to open{\n}treasure chests found on the battlefield.
genk16_50.18: {TIME:00}Try mixing the two Bomb Cores you{\n}{TIME:00}just found to see what happens.

hiku15_00.9: {MCR:s06l29:"Cid"}{\n}This is it! I’m hitting{\n}it with all we got!
hiku15_00.10: {MCR:s06l29:"Cid"}{\n}We’re out of ammo?{\n}This really isn’t my day...
hiku15_00.11: Move away from Evrae on {MCR:s06l29:"Cid"}’s turn
hiku15_00.24: {TIME:00}{CLR:WHITE}Use the trigger commands {CLR:YELLOW}Move in{CLR:WHITE} and {CLR:YELLOW}Pull back{CLR:WHITE} to{\n}adjust the distance between the airship and Evrae.{\n}Only {PC:00:TIDUS} and Rikku can give these orders.

kino02_00.6: {TIME:00}{PC:00:TIDUS}{\n}It’s gonna do something!{\n}Time for a head shot!

klyt00_50.4: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}Normally, it’s a skill that weakens{\n}enemies and heals the user.
klyt00_50.5: {PC:05:LULU}{\n}But when a Ronso uses it, that Ronso{\n}can sometimes learn fiends’ abilities.

klyt01_00.0: {TIME:00}{PC:00:TIDUS}{\n}It’s hard as rock!{\n}My sword’s no good!
klyt01_00.1: {TIME:00}{PC:04:WAKKA}{\n}My ball can’t hurt it, either!{\n}How ’bout magic!?
klyt01_00.2: {TIME:00}{PC:05:LULU}{\n}Its tentacles absorb{\n}any spells cast at its body!

maca02_00.7: {TIME:00}{PC:04:WAKKA}{\n}Stupid machina!{\n}They sealed their magic away, too!
maca02_00.10: {TIME:00}{PC:06:RIKKU}{\n}{PC:01:YUNA}! You can use magic{\n}and call aeons now!

mcfr03_00.0: {TIME:00}{PC:05:LULU}{\n}It absorbs all spells{\n}except its one weakness!

mcyt06_00.0: {TIME:00}{PC:00:TIDUS}{\n}I knew you were bad news{\n}the first time we met!
mcyt06_00.3: {TIME:00}{PC:01:YUNA}{\n}You may be a maester,{\n}but I will still fight!
mcyt06_00.7: {TIME:00}{PC:07:SEYMOUR}{\n}That power that defeated {PC:0D:ANIMA}...{\n}It will be mine!

menumain.1: {TIME:00}The cursor appears at the selected{\n}character’s current position.
menumain.3: {TIME:00}Information on upgrades is displayed at the top{\n}of the screen. This location is for {CLR:YELLOW}Defense upgrades{CLR:WHITE}
menumain.4: {TIME:00}while this one lets you {CLR:YELLOW}learn an Ability{CLR:WHITE}.{\n}
menumain.7: {TIME:00}A character must move,{\n}near a node in order to{\n}activate it.
menumain.10: {TIME:00}The cursor returns to the character’s position{\n}and a list of commands appear.
menumain.12: {TIME:00}The number of steps the character{\n}can move is displayed.
menumain.13: {TIME:00}The number of steps a character can move is{\n}equivalent to his {CLR:YELLOW}S. Lv. (Sphere Level){CLR:WHITE}.
menumain.14: {TIME:00}S. Lv. are gained by getting{\n}AP (Ability Points), which are obtained by{\n}participating in battles.
menumain.16: {TIME:00}In this case, {PC:00:TIDUS} has 4 {CLR:YELLOW}S. Lv.{CLR:WHITE},{\n}so he can move up to {CLR:YELLOW}4{CLR:WHITE} steps.
menumain.17: {TIME:00}In general, you should be careful not to rush{\n}and move too fast or you may forget to{\n}activate nodes you pass over.
menumain.19: {TIME:00}and learn the ability Cheer.{\n}Note that you can {CLR:YELLOW}activate{\n}nodes by being beside one{CLR:WHITE}.
menumain.21: {TIME:00}S. Lv. are spent and the character{\n}moves.
menumain.22: {TIME:00}When the character arrives at the designated spot,
menumain.23: {TIME:00}you are asked if you are surethis is the right destination.
menumain.25: {TIME:00}At this point, the movement processis complete and cannot be canceled.
menumain.26: {TIME:00}If you want to activate nodes{\n}you passed over, you must spend{\n}S. LV. and go back.
menumain.29: {TIME:00}Now that the character is in place,{\n}let’s {CLR:YELLOW}activate{CLR:WHITE} a node.
menumain.31: {TIME:00}Spheres are items that can be obtained by{\n}various means, mainly by defeating enemies.
menumain.34: {TIME:00}A list of spheres currently in stock{\n}is displayed.
menumain.36: {TIME:00}For example, {CLR:YELLOW}Power Spheres{CLR:WHITE}{\n}are used to activate Strength, Defense,{\n}and HP nodes.
menumain.39: {TIME:00}In this example, the only node that{\n}can be activated is an Ability  node,{\n}so the only Ability Spheres can be used.
menumain.41: {TIME:00}the nodes that can be activated by that{\n}sphere at that position are highlighted.
menumain.42: {TIME:00}Having selected Ability Spheres here, the node{\n}that is highlighted is the one Ability node.
menumain.43: {TIME:00}By slecting the node{\n}you wish to activate,
menumain.45: {TIME:00}Use the same procedure to improve the{\n}characters’ Strength, Magic, max HP & MP.
menumain.46: {TIME:00}Power Spheres, Magic Spheres,{\n}and Speed Spheres are needed to{\n}improve each corresponding stat.
menumain.47: {TIME:00}Also, every node on the Grid can be{\n}{CLR:YELLOW}activated separetely by each character{CLR:WHITE}.
menumain.48: {TIME:00}This means that a character can re-activate{\n}a node already used by another character by{\n}moving to it and using the corresponding sphere.
menumain.49: {TIME:00}Note that {CLR:YELLOW}Locks{CLR:WHITE} cannot be opened{\n}at present. Another tutorial will become{\n}available once you are able to open them.
menumain.50: {TIME:00}Now that you know how to upgrade your{\n}characters, let’s look at the other{\n}useful features of the Sphere Grid.
menumain.51: {TIME:00}The character’s current status can be{\n}dispayed by pressing {CTRL:30:TRIANGLE}.
menumain.52: {TIME:00}Use this information to plan{\n}how you wish to upgrade your{\n}characters.
menumain.55: {TIME:00}This is useful when you wish to compare{\n}the characters’ stats or when you wish to{\n}quickly upgrade multiple characters.
menumain.56: {TIME:00}Press {CTRL:39:SELECT} to zoom in and out{\n}for a better view of the Grid.
menumain.57: {TIME:00}This concludes the Sphere Grid tutorial.{\n}Try improving {PC:00:TIDUS} now.
menumain.58: {TIME:00}Some areas of the Sphere Grid are {CLR:YELLOW}locked{\n}by special nodes{CLR:WHITE}.
menumain.60: {TIME:00}You need to use special spheres{\n}called {CLR:YELLOW}Key Spheres{CLR:WHITE}.
menumain.62: {TIME:00}You must use {CLR:YELLOW}Lv. 1 Key Spheres{CLR:WHITE},{\n}as in this example...
menumain.65: {TIME:00}Use the 4 types of Key Spheres{\n}to unlock the corresponding 4 types{\n}of Locks.
menumain.66: {TIME:00}{CLR:YELLOW}Customize{CLR:WHITE}is used to upgrade{\n}weapons and armor by adding{\n}abilities to them.
menumain.67: {TIME:00}Items are needed to Customize and{\n}{CLR:YELLOW}add abilities to gear{CLR:WHITE}.
menumain.69: {TIME:00}Note that some weapons{\n}and armor cannot be customized.
menumain.70: {TIME:00}If any of the slots in the Abilities{\n}window shown at the bottom left of{\n}the screen is {CLR:YELLOW}empty{CLR:WHITE},
menumain.72: {TIME:00}If a gear has no empty slot,{\n}it cannot be customized.
menumain.73: {TIME:00}There are also gear that cannot{\n}be customized no matter what.
menumain.76: {TIME:00}a list of available abilities{\n}for that gear is displayed.
menumain.77: {TIME:00}The amount of items needed for the procedure{\n}is also displayed at the top right of the screen.
menumain.78: {TIME:00}On the left is the amount of items currently in stock,
menumain.79: {TIME:00}and on the left the amount of items left after the procedure.{\n}{CLR:YELLOW}Items are spent when Customizing gear{CLR:WHITE}.
menumain.80: {TIME:00}Abilities for which items are insufficient{\n}are displayed in {CLR:GREY}gray{CLR:WHITE},
menumain.82: {TIME:00}When you select an ability to add,
menumain.83: {TIME:00}you are asked to confirm the procedure.
menumain.88: {TIME:00}allows you to {CLR:YELLOW}make aeons{\n}learn abilities{CLR:WHITE}{\n}by spending items.
menumain.92: {TIME:00}The item cost for each{\n}ability is also displayed at{\n}the top right of the screen.
menumain.93: {TIME:00}On the left is the amount of items currently in stock,
menumain.94: {TIME:00}and on the right the amount of items left after the procedure.{\n}{CLR:YELLOW}Items are spent when aeons learn abilities{CLR:WHITE}.
menumain.96: {TIME:00}and abilities for which items are{\n}insufficient are displayed in {CLR:GREY}gray{CLR:WHITE}
menumain.98: {TIME:00}When you select an ability{\n}you want the aeon to learn,
menumain.99: {TIME:00}you are asked to confirm the procedure.
menumain.100: {TIME:00}Select ”Yes.” to teach the ability{\n}to the aeon.
menumain.101: {TIME:00}Aeons can learn most of the{\n}abilities the characters can,
menumain.102: {TIME:00}but there are some abilities{\n}that aeons can’t learn.
menumain.108: {TIME:00}The selected aeon’s stats are displayed.{\n}Stats that cannot be raised are{\n}shown in {CLR:GREY}gray{CLR:WHITE}
menumain.109: {TIME:00}The current status of each{\n}stat are the numbers on{\n}the right.
menumain.111: {TIME:00}When you choos a status to raise,{\n}the item cost for the procedure is{\n}displayed at the top right of the screen.
menumain.112: {TIME:00}The item costs differ depending{\n}on that aeon’s current stats.
menumain.113: {TIME:00}The item cost for raising stats is proportional{\n}to each stat’s value. The higher the stat,{\n}the more items are needed to raise it.
menumain.115: {TIME:00}you are asked to confirm the procedure.

stbv01_10.1: {TIME:00}Seymour{\n}So you, too, seek freedom{\n}from this painful life?
stbv01_10.3: {TIME:00}Seymour{\n}It is good to see you again, Lady Yuna,{\n}but you don’t seem pleased...
stbv01_10.4: {TIME:00}Yuna{\n}I’ll only be pleased when{\n}you’re gone to the Farplane!
stbv01_10.5: {TIME:00}Auron{\n}Although he was not{\n}the man I once knew...
stbv01_10.6: {TIME:00}Auron{\n}Kinoc was still my friend, Seymour.{\n}You will pay for his death!

znkd08_00.1: {PC:02:AURON}{\n}Don’t bother going after all of them.{\n}Cut the ones that matter, and run!

zzzz03_20.6: {TIME:00}{CLR:WHITE}Its strength is high, so use{\n}{CLR:OL_CYAN}Dark Attack{CLR:WHITE} to blind it.
zzzz03_20.19: {TIME:00}{CLR:WHITE}Xiphos has a high strength, so{\n}{CLR:OL_CYAN}Power Break{CLR:WHITE} is recommended.{\n}{CLR:OL_CYAN}Dark Buster{CLR:WHITE} is also effective.
zzzz03_20.25: {TIME:00}{CLR:WHITE}Cactuar tries to escape as soon as its{\n}HP gets low. {CLR:OL_CYAN}Sleep{CLR:WHITE} won’t work, so find{\n}other means of keeping it from running.
zzzz03_20.27: {TIME:00}{CLR:WHITE}Coeurl uses lots of magic, so use{\n}{CLR:OL_CYAN}Silence Buster{CLR:WHITE} or weapons that{\n}inflict {CLR:OL_CYAN}silence{CLR:WHITE} when they hit.
zzzz03_20.31: {TIME:00}{CLR:WHITE}Damage Bashura enough, and it will strike{\n}back hard, unless you attack with magic.{\n}{CLR:OL_CYAN}Sleep Buster{CLR:WHITE} and {CLR:OL_CYAN}Dark Buster{CLR:WHITE} are effective.
